summary: Bangladesh observes Eid al-Adha amid mixed circumstances, with Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina calling for charitable meat sharing and security forces on high alert, though celebrations are dampened by natural disasters and economic hardship. A sea-truck storm in the Meghna River narrowly avoided mass casualties, while a fatal bus crash killed four and a devastating garment factory fire highlighted workplace safety failures. Severe weather including heavy rainfall and earthquakes disrupted normal activities, and agricultural distress leaves many farmers unable to afford holiday observances, while a measles outbreak threatens to dampen Eid travel plans. The government faces scrutiny over corruption allegations and fiscal pressures, with bank borrowing exceeding annual targets while several infrastructure and health sector facilities report serious operational deficiencies.
